title: prunus pensylvanica subsp. corymbulosa
intro: Prunus pensylvanica subsp. corymbulosa, commonly known as Pin Cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its small, red or pink flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Pin Cherry include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming Pin Cherry fruits and leaves include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing relief from fever and respiratory issues. Pin Cherry can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
